0.q: Under normal growing conditions, the level of H2O2 was significantly increased in the transgenic lines when compared with that of WT. All of these results indicate that the PAO-mediated PAs catabolism accounted for the production of H2O2 in the transgenic plants.

1.q: HPLC analysis showed that when Spd and Spm were incubated in the CsPAO reaction system, a noticeable increase in Dap, concomitant with a decrease of Spd and Spm, suggesting that CsPAO4 was involved in PAs terminal catabolism reactions.

2.q: Heterologous expression of CsKCS6 in Arabidopsis significantly increased the amount of VLCFAs in the cuticular wax on the stems and leaves

3.q: Heterologous expression of CsKCS6 in Arabidopsis significantly increased the amount of VLCFAs in the cuticular wax on the stems and leaves.

4.q: two ATGs of sweet orange, CsATG18a and CsATG18b, were cloned and overexpressed in Arabidopsis ... ATG genes are known to be involved in the autophagy process

5.q: CsKCS6 not only plays an important role in the synthesis of fatty acid precursors involved in wax synthesis but also enhances the tolerance of transgenic Arabidopsis plants to abiotic stress.

6.q: Galactinol synthase (GolS) catalyzes the first and rate-limiting step in the synthesis of raffinose family of oligosaccharides (RFOs)
